title: Lawful Basis for Processing
description: Personal data is processed only with a valid lawful basis such as consent, contract, legal obligation, or legitimate interest.

title: Data Minimization
description: Only personal data that is adequate, relevant, and limited to what is necessary for the stated purpose is collected and processed.

title: Purpose Limitation
description: Personal data is collected for specified, explicit, and legitimate purposes and not further processed in a manner incompatible with those purposes.

title: Data Subject Rights
description: Mechanisms exist for data subjects to exercise their rights including access, rectification, erasure, and data portability.

title: Privacy by Design
description: Data protection is integrated into the design of processing activities and business practices from the outset.

title: Data Protection Impact Assessment
description: A DPIA is conducted for processing operations likely to result in high risk to the rights and freedoms of individuals.

title: Data Processing Agreements
description: Written agreements with data processors define the scope, nature, and purpose of processing and ensure GDPR compliance.

title: Breach Notification
description: Personal data breaches are reported to the supervisory authority within 72 hours and to affected individuals without undue delay.

title: Cross-Border Data Transfers
description: Transfers of personal data outside the EEA are protected by appropriate safeguards such as SCCs or adequacy decisions.

title: Automated Decision-Making Safeguards
description: Individuals are informed about and can contest decisions made solely by automated processing that significantly affect them.
